description: Implement all 9 UI/UX audit fixes across CSS and TSX files. These are all CSS/layout changes that do not affect TypeScript types.
steps: FIX 1 - Active sidebar icon invisible: In globals.css, change `.sidebar-nav-item.active .nav-icon { color: var(--accent); }` to use `color: var(--text-primary);` instead. The var(--accent) in globals.css (#0099ff) gets overridden by theme.css's --accent (#1c1c1c), making the icon invisible against the dark bg-elevated background.

FIX 2 - Ask OSAI card text truncation: In globals.css, add `padding-bottom: 16px;` to the `.ask-mode-card` rule (currently padding: 24px, change to include extra bottom space). Also add `position: relative; z-index: 1;` to `.ask-mode-card-desc` to ensure text sits above any gradient overlay. The gradient backgrounds on the cards can obscure the bottom text.

FIX 3 - Low contrast on status badges: In globals.css, update `.badge-red` to use a lighter, more translucent background: `background: rgba(255, 85, 119, 0.15);` (slightly more opaque than current 0.14 for better readability) and ensure the text color stays `color: var(--red);`. The key fix is making the border more visible: `border: 1px solid rgba(255, 85, 119, 0.35);`. This improves the contrast ratio to meet WCAG AA.

FIX 4 - Search bar discoverability: In globals.css, update `.topbar-search` to use `background: var(--bg-elevated);` (was var(--bg-surface)), add `border: 1px solid var(--border-hover);` (was var(--border)), and change `.topbar-search-text` to `color: var(--text-secondary);` (was var(--text-muted)). Add a focus-within rule: `.topbar-search:focus-within { border-color: var(--accent); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--accent-dim); }`.

FIX 5 - Dismiss action link looks disabled: In globals.css, change `.attention-card-action.dismiss` from `color: var(--text-muted);` to `color: var(--text-secondary);` and add hover state `.attention-card-action.dismiss:hover { color: var(--text-primary); }`. Also in inbox/page.tsx, the Dismiss button (context-item-actions) already uses btn-ghost class which is fine. But in globals.css for the attention-card-action.dismiss specifically, upgrade to text-secondary.

FIX 6 - Date column wrapping in Decision Log: In decisions/page.tsx, find the `<SortableTh label="Date" k="date" />` and add a width prop: `<SortableTh label="Date" k="date" width={130} />`. This ensures dates like 'May 30, 2026' fit on one line.

FIX 7 - Vertical misalignment in topbar: In globals.css, update `.topbar-workspace` to explicitly set `display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;` (currently gap: 6px). The display:flex and align-items:center are already present, just update gap to 8px for consistent spacing.

FIX 8 - Squished margins on Team tabs: In globals.css, update `.tabs-underline` margin-bottom from `22px` to `24px` and add `padding-bottom: 2px;` to give more breathing room between the active underline and the content below.

FIX 9 - Undefined visual states in Org Graph legend: In graph/page.tsx, add a 'No Access' entry to the tier legend display. After the existing tier legend dots, add a span showing a dash with 'No Access' label in the same style as the other legend items.
acceptance_criteria: Active sidebar nav icons use var(--text-primary) color - visible white on the dark elevated background
Ask OSAI workflow cards have extra bottom padding so text is not obscured by gradients
badge-red has improved contrast with slightly more opaque background and stronger border
Topbar search bar uses bg-elevated background and border-hover border for better visibility
Attention card dismiss action uses text-secondary color (brighter than text-muted)
Decision Log date column has width: 130 so dates don't wrap
Topbar workspace uses gap: 8px for consistent vertical alignment
Tabs underline has 24px bottom margin for breathing room
Org Graph legend includes a No Access entry with dash symbol
